title: No SQLite / embedded DB — storage is a plain in-memory array
evidence: src/database/Database.ts:17-95 stores books in a private `BookRow[]` (default DB_PATH=':memory:'); optional persistence is hand-rolled JSON via fs.writeFileSync (Database.ts:32-36). package.json dependencies list only express — no sqlite/better-sqlite3/sql.js. README states 'In-memory storage'.
suggestion: Use better-sqlite3 (or sql.js) with a books table and parameterized queries, or at minimum a real embedded DB, per the task's technical constraint.

title: Integration tests share a module-singleton DB with no per-test reset — count assertions are order-dependent
evidence: tests/integration.test.ts uses the module-singleton `db` (src/database/Database.ts:95 `export const db = new DatabaseManager()`) but only clears require.cache once in beforeAll (integration.test.ts:6-20); there is no beforeEach/afterEach reset. By the time 'should list all books' runs, the earlier POST test (line 40) has already inserted a book, so `expect(response.body.length).toBe(2)` (line 114) and the author-filter count (line 135) observe accumulated state and fail. Consistent with the low stored test_coverage=0.1761.
suggestion: Reset the store between tests (beforeEach re-import or a db.clear()), or assert on relative counts / created ids rather than absolute lengths.

title: POST /books over-validates — rejects requests missing year or isbn though spec only requires title+author
evidence: src/models/Book.ts:43-49 makes `year` and `isbn` mandatory (400 on absence), but TASK.md/R9 state only title and author are required.
suggestion: Make year and isbn optional in validateBookInput to match the 'title and author are required' constraint.

title: Unused, mismatched jest mock references a SQLite API that does not exist in the code
evidence: tests/__mocks__/database.ts:3-13 mocks a SQLite-style connection (exec/all/get/run) that the actual Database.ts (a JSON array) never exposes; no test calls jest.mock('../src/database/Database'), so the mock is dead and misleading.
suggestion: Delete the unused mock or align it with the real repository/db interface.

title: Low idiomatic score (0.38) for the target stack
evidence: scores.json idiomatic=0.38; hand-rolled JSON persistence and a repository that merely forwards to a singleton (src/database/BookRepository.ts:5-27) are non-idiomatic for an Express+SQLite service.
suggestion: Adopt a real DB layer and prepared statements to raise idiomatic quality.
